comment_text.sn22.58:1.3: The mendicant “freed by wisdom” is an arahant. Here it means any arahant, but sometimes it distinguishes one who has prioritized wisdom in their development (eg. MN 70:16.2).

root_text.sn22.58:1.2: “Tathāgato, bhikkhave, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho rūpassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto sammāsambuddhoti vuccati.
root_text.sn22.58:1.3: Bhikkhupi, bhikkhave, paññāvimutto rūpassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto paññāvimuttoti vuccati.
root_text.sn22.58:2.1: Tathāgato, bhikkhave, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vedanāya nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto sammāsambuddhoti vuccati.
root_text.sn22.58:2.2: Bhikkhupi, bhikkhave, paññāvimutto vedanāya nibbidā …pe… paññāvimuttoti vuccati.
root_text.sn22.58:3.3: viññāṇassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto sammāsambuddhoti vuccati.
root_text.sn22.58:3.4: Bhikkhupi, bhikkhave, paññāvimutto viññāṇassa nibbidā virāgā nirodhā anupādā vimutto paññāvimuttoti vuccati.
root_text.sn22.58:4.1: Tatra kho, bhikkhave, ko viseso, ko adhippayāso, kiṁ nānākaraṇaṁ, tathāgatassa arahato sammāsambuddhassa paññāvimuttena bhikkhunā”ti?
root_text.sn22.58:4.2: “Bhagavaṁmūlakā no, bhante, dhammā bhagavaṁnettikā bhagavaṁpaṭisaraṇā. Sādhu vata, bhante, bhagavantaññeva paṭibhātu etassa bhāsitassa attho. Bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.sn22.58:4.3: “Tena hi, bhikkhave, suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn22.58:4.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.sn22.58:5.1: “Tathāgato, bhikkhave, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho anuppannassa maggassa uppādetā, asañjātassa maggassa sañjanetā, anakkhātassa maggassa akkhātā maggaññū, maggavidū, maggakovido;
root_text.sn22.58:5.2: maggānugā ca, bhikkhave, etarahi sāvakā viharanti pacchā samannāgatā.
root_text.sn22.58:5.3: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso, ayaṁ adhippayāso, idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ tathāgatassa arahato sammāsambuddhassa paññāvimuttena bhikkhunā”ti.

translation_text.sn22.58:1.2: “Mendicants, a Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha is freed by not grasping, due to disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding form. They’re called a fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.sn22.58:1.3: A mendicant freed by wisdom is also freed by not grasping, due to disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding form. They’re called a mendicant freed by wisdom.
translation_text.sn22.58:2.1: A Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha is freed by not grasping, due to disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding feeling …
translation_text.sn22.58:3.3: consciousness. They’re called a fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.sn22.58:3.4: A mendicant freed by wisdom is also freed by not grasping, due to disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding consciousness. They’re called a mendicant freed by wisdom.
translation_text.sn22.58:4.1: What, then, is the difference between a Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha, and a mendicant freed by wisdom?”
translation_text.sn22.58:4.2: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. He is our guide and our refuge. Sir, may the Buddha himself please clarify the meaning of this. The mendicants will listen and remember it.”
translation_text.sn22.58:4.3: “Well then, mendicants, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn22.58:5.1: “The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha gave rise to the unarisen path, gave birth to the unborn path, and explained the unexplained path. He is the knower of the path, the discoverer of the path, the expert on the path.
translation_text.sn22.58:5.2: And now the disciples live following the path; they acquire it later.
translation_text.sn22.58:5.3: This is the difference between a Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha, and a mendicant freed by wisdom.”

variant_text.sn22.58:4.1: adhippayāso → adhippāyo (bj); adhippāyaso (sya-all, km, mr); adhippāyoso (pts1ed)
